title: witness-coverage/v1
description: in-toto Statement v1.0 carrying a witness branch-coverage predicate. Witness emits an unwrapped Statement (JSON); sigil wraps it in a DSSE envelope and signs it. The Statement's subject is the instrumented Wasm module; the predicate body carries the coverage report, measurement metadata, and an optional original-module digest. Derived from witness-core/src/predicate.rs.

subject.description: Subjects this Statement attests about. Subject 0 is the instrumented Wasm; subject 1 (when present) is the pre-instrumentation module.

predicateType.description: Predicate type URL identifying this document. Always `https://pulseengine.eu/witness-coverage/v1`.

Subject.description: in-toto Subject — name + cryptographic digest of an attestation target.

Digests.description: Cryptographic digests of an artefact. v0.10.0 uses SHA-256 only; additional algorithms may land later as additional fields.

CoveragePredicate.description: Predicate body — coverage report + measurement metadata + optional pre-instrumentation digest.

original_module.description: Pre-instrumentation module reference. Populated when the instrument step recorded the original SHA-256 in the manifest (v0.10.0+) and the predicate-build path passed it through. `null` when no chain back to the source module is available.

harness.description: Free-text label naming the harness that produced the run (e.g. "cargo test", "witness run"). `null` when not supplied.

measured_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp. Honours SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH (Reproducible-Builds spec) for envelope reproducibility.

witness_version.description: Witness toolchain version string (Cargo package version).

Report.description: Branch-coverage summary derived from the run record. Per-function and uncovered-branch lists are sorted deterministically.

module.description: Path of the instrumented Wasm module. Project-relative when path stripping is active (v0.10.0).

BranchKind.description:
Kind of branch a counter is counting.
- `br_if` — counter fires when the br_if branch is taken.
- `if_then` — counter fires when the consequent of an if/else runs.
- `if_else` — counter fires when the alternative of an if/else runs.
- `br_table_target` — counter fires when br_table dispatches to a specific target arm.
- `br_table_default` — counter fires when br_table takes its default arm.
